subtitle: Portfolio Governance & Agile Delivery, One Seat
hero_title:
Demand intake owner
and Scrum Master.
Same seat.
hero_sub: 10+ years bridging business stakeholders, Product Owners, IT teams, and executive leadership — managing the demand pipeline from intake through governance approval, then running the Agile ceremonies that turn an approved initiative into delivered work.

the_case.need: Demand intake collection, review, and organization with business justification and strategic alignment
the_case.answer: At Eptura, managed technology demand intake across an Agile PMO — collecting and evaluating initiative requests, assessing business justification and strategic alignment, and consolidating IT capacity and dependency analysis to support leadership prioritization.
the_case.detail: This is the single closest direct analog to the target role in my entire career — the same function, at enterprise SaaS scale, across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California.

the_case.need: Operations Board support: preparing and presenting technology demand portfolios
the_case.answer: Prepared and presented portfolio reporting to senior leadership with business, technical, and resource context at Eptura. At Crocs, ran go/no-go governance — readiness checkpoints, CAB-style change approval — the operational equivalent of Operations Board facilitation.
the_case.detail: Every decision was documented and communicated to all parties — not verbal alignment that evaporates after the meeting.

the_case.need: Scrum Master ceremonies, PO partnership, impediment removal, coaching
the_case.answer: Deep Scrum Master practice throughout a 15-year career — facilitating ceremonies, coaching Product Owners on backlog discipline, removing impediments, and monitoring delivery metrics.
the_case.detail: At Eptura, I served as Scrum Master for assigned product teams simultaneously with owning PMO-wide demand intake — the same dual function this role asks for, already proven in one seat.

the_case.need: Stakeholder alignment across business, Product Owners, IT, and executive leadership
the_case.answer: Trusted liaison between business and technology throughout my career — communicating risk, capacity constraints, and portfolio status clearly to senior leadership at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org.
the_case.detail: At Understood.org, I was the primary liaison between engineering leadership and executive stakeholders across a 12+-country distributed program.

the_case.need: Post-approval coordination: transitioning approved initiatives into backlog development and delivery
the_case.answer: At Eptura, facilitated prioritization/sequencing decisions and communicated approved priorities to Product Owners and delivery teams for transition into execution, maintaining transparency into the demand pipeline throughout.
the_case.detail: Governance without a clean handoff to delivery is just a meeting — I own the transition, not just the decision.

the_stack.headline: The governance & delivery toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily to keep demand, capacity, and delivery visible in one place.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Demand pipeline tracking, dashboards, AI-assisted reporting
nodes.pm_role: Built PMO-wide demand and delivery dashboards at Eptura across ~10 teams; embedded Jira AI at Crocs for a ~30% administrative overhead reduction.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.pm_role: Consolidated status, risk, dependency, and capacity data into stakeholder-ready dashboards at Crocs.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.desc: Governance documentation, decision records, portfolio reporting
nodes.pm_role: Used across every engagement for RAID logs and executive-facing portfolio reporting.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. Embedded Copilot directly into governance reporting workflows at Crocs.

when_things_break.scenario: Leadership can't see the full demand pipeline, capacity, and risk picture in one place
response: At Eptura, built Jira, Azure DevOps, and Smartsheet dashboards leadership could read without my narration
Consolidated IT capacity, dependency analysis, and demand intake into a single integrated view
Kept the system current as a living artifact, not a one-time snapshot
Result: leadership made prioritization decisions with real data, not guesswork

when_things_break.scenario: A release governance process is slowing delivery without actually reducing risk
response: At Crocs, ran go/no-go governance with readiness checkpoints and CAB-style change approval
Embedded Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot into the reporting workflow
Cut administrative overhead ~30% while making the output sharper, not slower
Result: on-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization

when_things_break.scenario: A distressed, multi-country program needs a single point of accountability fast
response: At Understood.org, became the primary liaison between engineering leadership and executive stakeholders across 12+ countries
Communicated delivery status, risk, and capacity constraints clearly at every level
Coordinated third-party vendors and tracked all post-approval commitments
Result: on-time launch with no critical regressions

how_i_run_a_room: Every intake request gets evaluated for business justification and strategic alignment before it reaches governance review
IT capacity and dependency data are consolidated continuously, not reconstructed for each board meeting
Governance decisions get documented and communicated to every stakeholder, not just the room that made them
Approved initiatives get a clean, tracked handoff into backlog development — nothing gets lost between approval and execution
Agile ceremonies produce real decisions and coaching, not just status updates
Risk and capacity constraints are communicated clearly at every level, from Product Owner to executive leadership

track_record.role: Agile Coach & Senior Scrum Master — Demand Intake & PMO Governance
points: Managed technology demand intake across an Agile PMO spanning ~10 distributed teams
Prepared and presented portfolio reporting to senior leadership
Served as Scrum Master for assigned product teams simultaneously
Drove 35%+ delivery efficiency gain, 20% sprint carryover reduction

track_record.role: Scrum Master & PM — Governance, Release Control & Delivery
points: Ran go/no-go governance cadence for a publicly traded retailer
Consolidated status, risk, and dependencies into stakeholder-ready dashboards
Partnered with Product Owner on intake business justification
Reduced administrative overhead ~30% via Jira AI and Copilot
Owned the full demand intake lifecycle solo, as founder
Assessed feasibility against capacity and dependencies
Sequenced work into delivery cycles and tracked commitments to resolution
Primary liaison between engineering leadership and executive stakeholders
Managed a distressed program across 12+ countries
Coordinated third-party vendors and post-approval commitments
Delivered on-time launch with no critical regressions

before: Demand requests pile up with no clear justification or prioritization process
Governance boards make decisions without full capacity or dependency visibility
Approved initiatives stall in the handoff between governance and delivery
Scrum ceremonies happen but don't connect back to portfolio-level priorities
after: Every intake request arrives with business justification and strategic alignment already assessed
Governance decisions are made with real-time capacity and dependency data
Approved initiatives transition cleanly into backlog development with nothing lost
Scrum ceremonies and portfolio governance operate as one connected system, not two

the_ramp.deliverable: Review the current demand intake pipeline, Operations Board cadence, and existing governance documentation; meet with Product Owners and IT leadership to understand current capacity constraints.
Begin facilitating ceremonies for assigned teams; identify the first gap in intake documentation or capacity visibility.

Own the full demand intake and Operations Board preparation cadence; deliver the first improved portfolio reporting format; establish working relationships with Product Owners, IT leadership, and executive stakeholders.
